title: Pre-program detection of threshold voltages of select gate transistors in a memory device
abstract: A memory device includes memory cells arranged in NAND strings between select gate transistors. A threshold voltage (Vth) distribution of the select gate transistors is evaluated, such as in response to a program, erase or read command involving a block or sub-block of memory cells. For example, a lower tail and an upper tail of the Vth distribution can be evaluated using read voltages. If the Vth is out-of-range, such as due to read disturb, data retention loss or defects in the memory device, the block or sub-block is marked as being bad and previously-programmed data in the block or sub-block can be copied to another location. If the Vth is in range, the command can be executed. Also, a control gate voltage for the select gate transistors can be set based on a Vth metric which is obtained from the evaluation.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A method for operating a memory device, comprising: receiving first instructions from a host device (140) at a memory device (210), the first instructions comprise a program command, write data and a first address, the first address identifies memory cells (203, 204, 207, 208, 500, 510, 520) in one plurality of NAND strings (NS0-NS68); and in response to the first instructions, evaluating a threshold voltage distribution (1200, 1201, 1202, 1205, 1210, 1215) of select gate transistors (201, 202, 205, 206) of the one plurality of NAND strings and determining, based on the evaluating, whether to execute the program command by programming the memory cells corresponding to the first address with the write data, or to return a program fail status to the host device without programming the memory cells corresponding to the first address with the write data. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein: if the program fail status is returned to the host device, storing data indicating that the one plurality of NAND strings is ineligible to store data, and copying previously- programmed data from the one plurality of NAND strings to another plurality of NAND strings which is not marked as being ineligible to store data. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein: the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises reading the select gate transistors using a read voltage (Vth_min, Vth_ltl, Vth_lt2, Vth_lt3, Vth_utl, Vth_ut2, Vth_ut3, Vth_ut4, Vth max) and, based on the reading, obtaining a count of the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage beyond the read voltage, wherein the program command is executed when the count is no more than a maximum allowable number, and the program fail status is returned to the host device when the count is more than the maximum allowable number. 4. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises: reading the select gate transistors using an upper read voltage (Vth_utl, Vth_ut2, Vth_ut3, Vth_ut4, Vth_max); based on the reading, obtaining a first count of the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage above the upper read voltage; reading the select gate transistors using a lower read voltage (Vth_min, Vth_ltl, Vth_lt2, Vth_lt3); and obtaining a second count of the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage below the lower read voltage, wherein the program command is executed when the first count is no more than a respective maximum allowable number and the second count is no more than a respective maximum allowable number. 5. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises: reading the select gate transistors using an upper read voltage (Vth_utl, Vth_ut2, Vth_ut3, Vth_ut4, Vth_max); based on the reading, obtaining a first count of the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage above the upper read voltage; determining a number of program-erase cycles in the memory device; if the number of program-erase cycles is more than a specified number, reading the select gate transistors using a lower read voltage (Vth_min, Vth_ltl, Vth_lt2, Vth_lt3) and obtaining a second count of the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage below the lower read voltage, wherein the program command is executed when the first count is no more than a respective maximum allowable number and the second count is no more than a respective maximum allowable number; and if the number of program-erase cycles is less than the specified number, omitting reading of the select gate transistors using the lower read voltage, wherein the program command is executed when the first count is no more than a respective maximum allowable number. 6. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises: reading a lower tail (1205a, 1210a, 1215a) of the threshold voltage distribution of the select gate transistors using a first plurality of read voltages (Vth_min, Vth_ltl, Vth_lt2, Vth_lt3); obtaining a threshold voltage metric of the lower tail based on the reading; reading an upper tail (1206a, 1211a, 1216a) of the threshold voltage distribution of the select gate transistors using a second plurality of read voltages; obtaining a threshold voltage metric of the upper tail based on the reading of the upper tail; and determining a control gate voltage (Vsgl, Vsg2) to apply to the select gate transistors during program voltages of the programming based on the threshold voltage metric of the lower tail and the threshold voltage metric of the upper tail. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising: selecting an initial read voltage of the second plurality of read voltages based on the threshold voltage metric of the lower tail or selecting an initial read voltage of the first plurality of read voltages based on the threshold voltage metric of the upper tail. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, further comprising, if the program fail status is returned to the host device: subsequently receiving second instructions from the host device at the memory device, the second instructions comprise another program command and a second address, the second address identifies memory cells in another plurality of NAND strings and in response to the second instructions: evaluating a threshold voltage distribution of select gate transistors of the another plurality of NAND strings; and determining, based on the evaluating of the threshold voltage distribution of the select gate transistors of the another plurality of NAND strings, whether to execute the another program command by programming the memory cells corresponding to the second address with the write data, or to return a program fail status to the host device without programming the memory cells corresponding to the second address with the write data. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein: the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises reading the select gate transistors to identify a specific select gate transistor having a threshold voltage outside a specified range, wherein the determining, based on the evaluating, whether to execute the program command considers a location of the specific select gate transistor in the memory device, wherein the location is a location in a select gate layer in a stack of alternating conductive and dielectric layers. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein: the evaluating the threshold voltage distribution comprises reading the select gate transistors to identify a number N>1 of adjacent select gate transistor having a threshold voltage outside a specified range, wherein the determining, based on the evaluating, whether to execute the program command assigns a higher weight to the N>1 adjacent select gate transistor having a threshold voltage outside the specified range than to N>1 non-adjacent select gate transistor having a threshold voltage outside the specified range. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, further comprising: saving a result of the evaluating, the result indicates the program command is to be executed; receiving second instructions from the host device at the memory device, the second instructions comprise a second program command, second write data and a second address, the second address identifies memory cells in the one plurality of NAND strings; and in response to the result, executing the second program command by programming the memory cells corresponding to the second address with the second write data without evaluating the threshold voltage distribution of the select gate transistors. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11, further comprising: if the program fail status is returned, evaluating a threshold voltage distribution of select gate transistors of another plurality of NAND strings, wherein the one plurality of NAND strings and the another plurality of NAND strings are in different sub-blocks (SB0- SB3) of a block (BLK). 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, further comprising: if the evaluating indicates that more than a specified number of the select gate transistors of the one plurality of NAND strings have an out-of-range threshold voltages, evaluating a threshold voltage distribution of select gate transistors of another plurality of NAND strings, wherein the one plurality of NAND strings and the another plurality of NAND strings are in different sub-blocks of a block. 14. A memory device, comprising: a plurality of NAND strings (NS0-NS68), each NAND string comprising a select gate transistor (201, 202, 205, 206) and memory cells (203, 204, 207, 208, 500, 510, 520), the select gate transistors having a threshold voltage distribution (1200, 1201, 1202, 1205, 1210, 1215); and a control circuit (220, 222, 224, 226, 240, 242, 230, 244), the control circuit is configured to: receive first instructions from a host device (140), the first instructions comprise a program command, write data and a first address, the first address identifies memory cells in one plurality of NAND strings; in response to the first instructions, read the select gate transistors using a plurality of read voltages (Vth_min, Vth_ltl, Vth_lt2, Vth_lt3, Vth_utl, Vth_ut2, Vth_ut3, Vth_ut4, Vth_max); based on the read of the select gate transistors, obtain a threshold voltage metric of a first tail (1205a, 1210a, 1215a; 1206a, 1211a, 1216a) of the threshold voltage distribution; and based on the threshold voltage metric of the first tail, determine a control gate voltage (Vsgl, Vsg2) to apply to the select gate transistors during program voltages of a programming operation in which the write data is programmed into the memory cells in response to the program command, wherein the control gate voltage is calculated as a function of the threshold voltage metric of the first tail and an offset. 15. The memory device of claim 14, wherein the control circuit is configured to: in response to the first instructions, read the select gate transistors using another plurality of read voltages; and 

 based on the read of the select gate transistors using the another plurality of read voltages, obtain a threshold voltage metric of a second tail of the threshold voltage distribution, wherein the control gate voltage is calculated as a function of the threshold voltage metric of the second tail.
